behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (dispatcher -> per-reviewer client payload), not model judgment. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network/subprocess is not required; in-process fakes still catch wrong doc reuse or wrong instruction-pack routing. LLM-DEP: behavior checks inputs sent, not output quality. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate (test payload-builder function directly) can miss wiring bugs where dispatcher maps wrong pack to role; Integration candidate (dispatcher + role adapters + fake reviewer endpoints) catches that mapping failure. Rejection gate: System/Agentic rejected because behavior text is 'sends ... correct ... instruction pack' (transport/config correctness only).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real dispatcher with three role adapters and fake reviewer endpoints that record requests. Send one contract. Assert all three requests contain byte-identical source document and each role gets its own expected instruction pack. This catches cross-role misrouting and shared-mutable-payload bugs.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model extraction quality and can vary run-to-run; stubbed outputs hide misses on legal phrasing. REAL-INFRA may be present, but the minimal boundary is model decision quality, so Agentic is the floor. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate with fixed stub response only verifies parser/schema handling and will always pass if fixture is correct; Agentic candidate with real extractor on labeled contracts catches semantic extraction failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live clause extractor on a curated contract set with gold labels for parties/dates/obligations/penalties. Assert recall/precision thresholds per field (or required hit list per contract). This catches true model misses on paraphrased or nested clauses.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is config propagation across components (policy loader -> orchestrator -> risk client). Unit test of a serializer alone can miss dropped/overridden fields in wiring. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed to catch this; fake assessor endpoint is enough. LLM-DEP: assertion is about sent config, not model quality. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is 'configuration is passed correctly'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-review path with a policy fixture containing multiple thresholds/rules and a fake risk assessor client that captures request payload. Assert payload includes exact policy version and rule set expected for that run. This catches stale/default-policy leakage and field-name mismatch.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: behavior explicitly requires semantic judgment on indirect/euphemistic language; mocks cannot validate that. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration with canned responses only checks plumbing; Agentic with real model + labeled examples catches false negatives on euphemisms. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because this is one reviewer’s decision, not multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run live risk assessor against a benchmark set containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses with expected policy outcomes. Assert detection metrics or minimum true-positive count on euphemistic subset. This catches real-model semantic blind spots.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: likely failure is handoff across config selection and compliance-invocation wiring. Unit-only coverage can miss wrong profile chosen per document context. REAL-INFRA: real network/process not necessary for this failure mode. LLM-DEP: behavior checks invocation inputs, not compliance reasoning output. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected by behavior text 'are passed correctly ... invocation'.
behaviors.test_description: Run compliance invocation path with a contract tagged for a specific jurisdiction and regulatory profile; use fake compliance client to capture request. Assert jurisdiction/profile identifiers and expanded requirement set match expected mapping. This catches context-to-profile routing bugs.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model/legal reasoning quality against requirements; deterministic stubs hide reasoning failures. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration stub test can verify requirement list is sent but cannot validate determination correctness; Agentic test with gold outcomes catches model misclassification. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because this behavior isolates one reviewer decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Run live compliance checker on a labeled suite of contracts spanning pass/fail and borderline cases per regulation set. Assert accuracy/recall floors for non-compliance detection. This catches real false-pass/false-fail decisions.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stated failure is merge logic behavior on conflicting inputs; can be reproduced with direct input objects to merge function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required for this specific rule. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate feeding conflicting findings into merge function catches overwrite/dedup bugs directly; Integration candidate adds dispatcher/reviewer mocks but does not increase failure detection for this merge rule. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text is solely about 'merge step preserves both ... with attribution' given existing flags.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function with two findings sharing clause ID but different assessments and reviewer identities. Assert merged report contains both assessments, each tagged with original reviewer attribution, with no collapse to a single value. This catches lossy conflict-resolution logic.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: includes multiple agentic reviewers in sequence with orchestration/collection/merge; correctness depends on several model-driven outputs being preserved through pipeline stages. LLM-DEP: each reviewer output is model-dependent. REAL-INFRA may exist, but Workflow is higher and required. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Agentic single-step test per reviewer cannot catch cross-stage loss during parallel collection+merge; Workflow E2E run catches dropped findings, race/aggregation bugs, and attribution loss across full pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline on real contracts where expected findings are known per reviewer. Capture each reviewer raw output and final merged report; assert every reviewer finding is represented in final report (or preserved as explicit conflict) with attribution. This catches fan-out/fan-in loss and merge omission under real parallel execution.
